The **British Figure Skating Championships** are an annual [figure skating competition](/wiki/Figure_skating_competition "Figure skating competition") organised by [British Ice Skating](/wiki/British_Ice_Skating "British Ice Skating") to crown the [national champions](/wiki/List_of_figure_skating_competitions "List of figure skating competitions") of Great Britain.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The first official British Championships were held in 1903 in [London](/wiki/London "London"). The competition – originally called the Swedish Challenge Cup – allowed for both men and women to compete, and consisted of [compulsory figures](/wiki/Compulsory_figures "Compulsory figures") and [free skating](/wiki/Free_skating "Free skating"); [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers") was the winner. [Pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating") was added to the championships in 1921, a separate competition for women in 1927, and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance") in 1937. They have been interrupted only three times since their inception.

Medals are awarded in [men's singles, women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), pair skating, and ice dance at the senior and junior levels, although not every discipline may be held every year due to a lack of participants. [John Page](/wiki/John_Page_(figure_skater) "John Page (figure skater)") currently holds the record for winning the most British Championship titles in men's singles (with eleven). In addition, Page and his partner [Ethel Muckelt](/wiki/Ethel_Muckelt "Ethel Muckelt") hold the record in pair skating (with nine). [Jenna McCorkell](/wiki/Jenna_McCorkell "Jenna McCorkell") holds the record in women's singles (with eleven), while [Lilah Fear](/wiki/Lilah_Fear "Lilah Fear") and [Lewis Gibson](/wiki/Lewis_Gibson_(figure_skater) "Lewis Gibson (figure skater)") hold the record ice dance (with eight).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=British_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The championships were originally called the **Swedish Challenge Cup** and were inaugurated by the National Skating Association of Great Britain in 1903, but were not established as the British Championships until 1906.[[2]](#cite_note-Times03021911-2) The Swedish Challenge Cup had been presented to Great Britain on behalf of the Stockholm General Skating Club ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Stockholms Allmänna Skridskoklubb*) by [Viktor Balck](/wiki/Viktor_Balck "Viktor Balck"), the then-president of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union").[[3]](#cite_note-Times02221906-3)

In the beginning, the British Championships were explicitly for skating in the [international style](/wiki/International_style_(figure_skating) "International style (figure skating)"), versus the [English style](/wiki/English_style_(figure_skating) "English style (figure skating)"), which had once been the standard in Great Britain.[[4]](#cite_note-Hines2006-4) The international style incorporated ballet and dance movements with ice skating and was championed by early British skaters like [Edgar Syers](/wiki/Edgar_Syers "Edgar Syers") and [Henry Yglesias](/wiki/Henry_Yglesias "Henry Yglesias").[[4]](#cite_note-Hines2006-4) The English style, on the other hand, was much more rigid and formal, and focussed on the tracing of elaborate [special figures](/wiki/Special_figures "Special figures"). The International Skating Union had adopted the international style as the standard for all international competitions in 1897.[[4]](#cite_note-Hines2006-4)

The inaugural competition in 1903 was won by [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers").[[5]](#cite_note-BG02251909-5) [Pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating") was added to the championships in 1921.[[6]](#cite_note-Guardian1921-6) Although women competed in the competition, figure skating was dominated by men at the time, so a separate category for women was established in 1927.[[7]](#cite_note-Guardian03141927-7) [Kathleen Shaw](/wiki/Kathleen_Shaw "Kathleen Shaw"), the winner of the inaugural women's event, stated: "It is much fairer now that there is a championship for women as well as for men... It is possible that ice skating will become more popular now that women are allowed their own championship."[[7]](#cite_note-Guardian03141927-7) [Ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance") became a championship event in 1937,[[8]](#cite_note-CopleyGraves-8) although the ice dance competitions were for many years held separately from the other events. From 1951 to 1987, the British Ice Dance Championships were held annually at the [Nottingham Ice Stadium](/wiki/Nottingham_Ice_Stadium "Nottingham Ice Stadium") in [Nottingham](/wiki/Nottingham "Nottingham").[[9]](#cite_note-Richardson-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Champs1987-10)

The British Championships were open to skaters from [members of the British Commonwealth](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Nations "Commonwealth of Nations"); skaters from Australia, Canada, and South Africa occasionally competed.[[11]](#cite_note-Guardian03131928-11)[[12]](#cite_note-Aussies-12)[[13]](#cite_note-BG12111946-13) They have been interrupted three times since their inception: between 1915 and 1920 due to [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"),[[4]](#cite_note-Hines2006-4) between 1940 and 1945 due to [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"),[[14]](#cite_note-SkatingOctober1946-14) and in 2021 due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/Impact_of_the_COVID-19_pandemic_on_sports#Figure_skating "Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports").[[15]](#cite_note-COVID19-15)

The reigning British figure skating champions: [Edward Appleby](/wiki/Edward_Appleby "Edward Appleby") ([men's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating")); [Kristen Spours](/wiki/Kristen_Spours "Kristen Spours") ([women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating")), [Anastasia Vaipan-Law](/wiki/Anastasia_Vaipan-Law "Anastasia Vaipan-Law") and [Luke Digby](/wiki/Luke_Digby "Luke Digby") ([pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating")); and [Lilah Fear](/wiki/Lilah_Fear "Lilah Fear") and [Lewis Gibson](/wiki/Lewis_Gibson_(figure_skater) "Lewis Gibson (figure skater)") ([ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance"))
